arguments.file_text:
# HelpChatbot ‚Äî Implementation Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based, LLM-driven chatbot using C# / Blazor that answers customer questions based on internal product PDF documentation. Use Postgres for data storage and a local `Docs/` folder for source PDFs.

## Approach ‚Äî Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
The chatbot will use the RAG pattern:
1. **Ingest** ‚Äî Extract text from PDFs in `Docs/`, split into chunks, generate embeddings, and store them in Postgres (pgvector).
2. **Query** ‚Äî When a user asks a question, embed the question, find the most relevant chunks via vector similarity search, and send them as context to an LLM to produce a grounded answer.

## Architecture

```
User ‚Üî Blazor Server UI ‚Üî ChatService ‚Üî LLM (OpenAI / Azure OpenAI)
                                ‚Üï
                    DocumentService (RAG)
                      ‚Üï            ‚Üï
               PDF Files      Postgres + pgvector
              (Docs/)        (chunks & embeddings)
```

## Tech Stack
| Concern | Choice |
|---|---|
| Web UI | Blazor Server (.NET 10) |
| LLM integration | Microsoft.Extensions.AI + OpenAI provider |
| PDF text extraction | UglyToad.PdfPig |
| Embeddings | OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` |
| Vector DB | PostgreSQL + pgvector extension |
| ORM / DB access | Npgsql + Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL |
| Vector support | Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore |

## Project Structure (target)

```
HelpChatbot/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Components/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ _Imports.razor
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ App.razor                  # Root component / HTML host
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Routes.razor               # Router
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Layout/
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ MainLayout.razor       # Shared layout
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ Pages/
‚îÇ       ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ Chat.razor             # Chat page UI
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Models/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ChatMessage.cs             # DB entity for conversation history
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ DocumentChunk.cs           # DB entity for embedded doc chunks
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Data/
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ ChatDbContext.cs           # EF Core DbContext (pgvector)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Services/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ChatService.cs             # Orchestrates RAG + LLM calls
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ DocumentService.cs         # PDF ingestion, chunking, embedding
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Docs/
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ product-guide.pdf          # Source documentation (already exists)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Program.cs                     # DI, middleware, startup
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ HelpChatbot.csproj
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ appsettings.json               # Connection strings, OpenAI key placeholder
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ wwwroot/
    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ css/
        ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ app.css                # Chat styles
```

## Todos

### 1. Add NuGet packages
Add dependencies: `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI`, `UglyToad.PdfPig`, `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL`, `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore`, Blazor interactive server components.

### 2. Create appsettings.json
Add configuration for Postgres connection string and OpenAI API key placeholders.

### 3. Create data models
- `DocumentChunk` ‚Äî id, file name, chunk index, text content, embedding vector.
- `ChatMessage` ‚Äî id, conversation id, role (user/assistant), content, timestamp.

### 4. Create EF Core DbContext
`ChatDbContext` with `DbSet<DocumentChunk>` and `DbSet<ChatMessage>`, pgvector column mapping.

### 5. Implement DocumentService
- Scan `Docs/` folder for PDFs.
- Extract text with PdfPig.
- Split text into ~500-token chunks with overlap.
- Call OpenAI embeddings API to embed each chunk.
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into Postgres.
- Provide a similarity-search method (cosine distance) to retrieve top-K relevant chunks for a query.

### 6. Implement ChatService
- Accept a user question and conversation id.
- Embed the question, call `DocumentService` to find relevant chunks.
- Build a prompt with system instructions + retrieved context + user question.
- Call the LLM via `IChatClient` (Microsoft.Extensions.AI).
- Save user + assistant messages to the database.
- Return the assistant's answer.

### 7. Build Blazor UI
- Set up Blazor Server interactive rendering (App.razor, Routes.razor, MainLayout).
- Create `Chat.razor` page with a message list, input box, and send button.
- Wire up to `ChatService`; display streaming responses.

### 8. Configure Program.cs & DI
- Register EF Core, `ChatService`, `DocumentService`, `IChatClient`, and embedding client.
- Add Blazor Server services and middleware.
- On startup, trigger document ingestion if the DB is empty / docs have changed.

### 9. Add CSS for the chat UI
Minimal, clean chat styling (message bubbles, input area, scrollable history).

### 10. Validate the build
Run `dotnet build` to confirm everything compiles without errors.

## Notes
- The OpenAI API key and Postgres connection string are stored as placeholders in `appsettings.json`; users must supply real values.
- The `Docs/` folder is scanned at startup; dropping new PDFs and restarting re-ingests.
- pgvector must be enabled on the Postgres server (`CREATE EXTENSION vector;`) ‚Äî the EF migration or startup code will handle this.
- Conversation history is persisted so the assistant can reference earlier messages in the same session.

Now I have a good understanding of the existing project. It's a bare-bones .NET 10 web app with a single PDF in the Docs folder. Let me create a comprehensive plan for building the LLM-driven chatbot.

Key components needed:
1. Blazor Server UI for the chat interface
2. PDF text extraction from the Docs folder
3. Postgres with pgvector for storing document embeddings (RAG pattern)
4. Integration with an LLM (e.g., OpenAI via Microsoft.Extensions.AI or Semantic Kernel)
5. RAG pipeline: extract PDF text ‚Üí chunk ‚Üí embed ‚Üí store in Postgres ‚Üí query with user question ‚Üí send context + question to LLM


6. A service layer to orchestrate the embedding and retrieval workflow, plus LLM calls

# HelpChatbot ‚Äî Implementation Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based, LLM-driven chatbot using C# / Blazor that answers customer questions based on internal product PDF documentation. Use Postgres for data storage and a local `Docs/` folder for source PDFs.

## Approach ‚Äî Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
The chatbot will use the RAG pattern:
1. **Ingest** ‚Äî Extract text from PDFs in `Docs/`, split into chunks, generate embeddings, and store them in Postgres (pgvector).
2. **Query** ‚Äî When a user asks a question, embed the question, find the most relevant chunks via vector similarity search, and send them as context to an LLM to produce a grounded answer.

## Architecture

```
User ‚Üî Blazor Server UI ‚Üî ChatService ‚Üî LLM (OpenAI / Azure OpenAI)
                                ‚Üï
                    DocumentService (RAG)
                      ‚Üï            ‚Üï
               PDF Files      Postgres + pgvector
              (Docs/)        (chunks & embeddings)
```

## Tech Stack
| Concern | Choice |
|---|---|
| Web UI | Blazor Server (.NET 10) |
| LLM integration | Microsoft.Extensions.AI + OpenAI provider |
| PDF text extraction | UglyToad.PdfPig |
| Embeddings | OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` |
| Vector DB | PostgreSQL + pgvector extension |
| ORM / DB access | Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL |
| Vector support | Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore |

## Project Structure (target)

```
HelpChatbot/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Components/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ _Imports.razor
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ App.razor                  # Root component / HTML host
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Routes.razor               # Router
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Layout/
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ MainLayout.razor       # Shared layout
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ Pages/
‚îÇ       ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ Chat.razor             # Chat page UI
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Models/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ChatMessage.cs             # DB entity for conversation history
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ DocumentChunk.cs           # DB entity for embedded doc chunks
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Data/
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ ChatDbContext.cs           # EF Core DbContext (pgvector)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Services/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ChatService.cs             # Orchestrates RAG + LLM calls
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ DocumentService.cs         # PDF ingestion, chunking, embedding
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Docs/
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ product-guide.pdf          # Source documentation (already exists)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Program.cs                     # DI, middleware, startup
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ HelpChatbot.csproj
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ appsettings.json               # Connection strings, OpenAI key placeholder
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ wwwroot/
    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ css/
        ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ app.css                # Chat styles
```

## Todos

### 1. Add NuGet packages
Add dependencies: `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI`, `UglyToad.PdfPig`, `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL`, `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore`, Blazor interactive server components.

### 2. Create appsettings.json
Add configuration for Postgres connection string and OpenAI API key placeholders.

### 3. Create data models
- `DocumentChunk` ‚Äî id, file name, chunk index, text content, embedding vector.
- `ChatMessage` ‚Äî id, conversation id, role (user/assistant), content, timestamp.

### 4. Create EF Core DbContext
`ChatDbContext` with `DbSet<DocumentChunk>` and `DbSet<ChatMessage>`, pgvector column mapping.

### 5. Implement DocumentService
- Scan `Docs/` folder for PDFs.
- Extract text with PdfPig.
- Split text into ~500-token chunks with overlap.
- Call OpenAI embeddings API to embed each chunk.
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into Postgres.
- Provide a similarity-search method (cosine distance) to retrieve top-K relevant chunks for a query.

### 6. Implement ChatService
- Accept a user question and conversation id.
- Embed the question, call `DocumentService` to find relevant chunks.
- Build a prompt with system instructions + retrieved context + user question.
- Call the LLM via `IChatClient` (Microsoft.Extensions.AI).
- Save user + assistant messages to the database.
- Return the assistant's answer.

### 7. Build Blazor UI
- Set up Blazor Server interactive rendering (App.razor, Routes.razor, MainLayout).
- Create `Chat.razor` page with a message list, input box, and send button.
- Wire up to `ChatService`; display streaming responses.

### 8. Configure Program.cs & DI
- Register EF Core, `ChatService`, `DocumentService`, `IChatClient`, and embedding client.
- Add Blazor Server services and middleware.
- On startup, trigger document ingestion if the DB is empty / docs have changed.

### 9. Add CSS for the chat UI
Minimal, clean chat styling (message bubbles, input area, scrollable history).

### 10. Validate the build
Run `dotnet build` to confirm everything compiles without errors.

## Notes
- The OpenAI API key and Postgres connection string are stored as placeholders in `appsettings.json`; users must supply real values.
- The `Docs/` folder is scanned at startup; dropping new PDFs and restarting re-ingests.
- pgvector must be enabled on the Postgres server (`CREATE EXTENSION vector;`) ‚Äî the EF migration or startup code will handle this.
- Conversation history is persisted so the assistant can reference earlier messages in the same session.

The chatbot will use the RAG pattern:
